proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean ~65, plausibly age in years) is positively correlated with pfs_months at the patient level.
feature_051 (binary, prevalence ~55%) is associated with shorter pfs_months than its absence (i.e., negative effect on the outcome).
feature_013 (heavily right-skewed continuous, median ~16, max >3000, plausibly a tumor-burden lab) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_006 (continuous, mean ~3.8) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_009 (continuous, mean ~3.8, max 5.5, plausibly serum albumin) is positively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.856, p ~ 0 across n=50000. Each year of feature_078 is associated with ~0.17 months longer pfs_months (slope from simple OLS). Direction: positive, very strong.
Mean pfs_months: 4.030 (feature_051=0) vs 3.509 (feature_051=1). Welch t = -28.85, p = 1.6e-181. Direction: feature_051=1 patients have shorter PFS by 0.52 months on average.
Pearson r = -0.153, p = 3.5e-260. Negative association: higher feature_013 -> shorter pfs_months. Skew in feature_013 means a log-transform is more appropriate (explored later).
Pearson r = -0.143, p = 9.9e-226. Negative association: higher feature_006 -> shorter pfs_months.
Pearson r = +0.123, p = 1.5e-166. Positive association: higher feature_009 -> longer pfs_months. Consistent with an albumin-like protective biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3-level ordinal, values 0/1/2, plausibly performance status) shows a monotonic negative association with pfs_months (each higher level has shorter PFS).
feature_067 (5-level ordinal, values 6-10, plausibly Gleason score) shows a negative association with pfs_months: higher feature_067 -> shorter PFS.
Other 5-level ordinal features (feature_071, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064, feature_016) are NOT meaningfully associated with pfs_months in unadjusted analysis (null hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: Group means: level 0 = 4.681 mo (n=17592), level 1 = 3.492 mo (n=24971), level 2 = 2.376 mo (n=7437). Univariate OLS slope on the linear coding = -1.158 months per level (p < 1e-300). Strong monotonic negative association.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_027 + log_f013 + log_f006 + feature_009 + feature_092 + C(feature_067)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate ANOVA across feature_067 levels (6,7,8,9,10) shows tiny mean differences (3.71, 3.76, 3.74, 3.74, 3.70). Adjusted for major predictors, levels 8/9/10 vs reference 6 have coefficients -0.023, -0.023, -0.019 (p<0.05); modest negative gradient consistent with Gleason. Effect is clinically small but directionally as predicted.

analyses.code: for c in ['feature_071','feature_096','feature_033','feature_064','feature_016']: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + ... + C({c})', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_071, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064: no level coefficient reached p<0.05 in adjusted model (all p>0.2). feature_016 had a single borderline level-2 coefficient (+0.012, p=0.013). Interpretation: these four ordinals carry little independent prognostic signal; null largely supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across racial/ethnic categories of feature_018 (white, black, hispanic, asian, other) in an unadjusted comparison.
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance categories of feature_045 (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured) in an unadjusted comparison.

analyses.result_summary: Means by feature_018: asian 3.797, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, other 3.749, white 3.741. One-way ANOVA F~0.7, p~0.6. Range of group means is only ~0.06 months (~1 day). Hypothesis NOT supported: no meaningful unadjusted PFS difference by race.

Means by feature_045: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. One-way ANOVA p~0.5. Range of group means ~0.03 months. Hypothesis NOT supported: no meaningful unadjusted PFS difference by insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for feature_078 (age), feature_057 (PS-like), log(feature_013), log(feature_006), feature_009, feature_092 and treatment indicators, race (feature_018) remains associated with pfs_months (REFINED from h9).
After the same adjustment, insurance (feature_045) remains associated with pfs_months (REFINED from h10).
Race and insurance jointly contribute > 0.1% incremental R^2 to a multivariable model of pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_027 + log_f013 + log_f006 + feature_009 + feature_092 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients (vs reference asian): black +0.003 (p=0.74), hispanic +0.002 (p=0.81), other +0.007 (p=0.61), white +0.002 (p=0.74). All non-significant; magnitudes <1 day. Hypothesis NOT supported.

Adjusted insurance coefficients (vs reference medicaid): medicare -0.0003 (p=0.95), private -0.00002 (p=1.00), uninsured +0.0006 (p=0.95). All non-significant; magnitudes effectively zero. Hypothesis NOT supported.

analyses.code: m_no = ols(...without race+insurance...); m_demo = ols(...with race+insurance...); F-test on 7 added df
analyses.result_summary: R^2 without demographics = 0.96792; R^2 with demographics = 0.96792. Incremental R^2 = 0.00000. Joint F-test for the 7 demographic dummies: F=0.043, p=0.9999. Hypothesis NOT supported. Race and insurance carry no signal once clinical predictors are accounted for.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features, scanning each in a model adjusted for feature_078, feature_057, log(feature_013), log(feature_006), feature_009 will reveal at least one previously-overlooked binary feature with a positive (PFS-prolonging) effect.
Specifically, feature_109 (prevalence ~10%) is positively associated with pfs_months in the adjusted model.
feature_039 (prevalence ~10%) is positively associated with pfs_months in the adjusted model.
feature_027 (prevalence ~60%) is positively associated with pfs_months in the adjusted model.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + log_f013 + log_f006 + feature_009 + {c}', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Two binaries jump out: feature_109 (coef +0.153, p=8.8e-169) and feature_039 (coef +0.147, p=1.4e-159). feature_027 also significant (+0.025, p=1.1e-13). Hypothesis supported.
feature_109 adjusted coefficient = +0.151 months (95% CI 0.141 to 0.162), p ~ 1e-168. Supported: patients with feature_109=1 gain ~0.15 months PFS controlling for clinical covariates.
feature_039 adjusted coefficient = +0.147 months (95% CI 0.136 to 0.157), p ~ 1.4e-159. Supported. Effect magnitude essentially identical to feature_109.
feature_027 adjusted coefficient = +0.025 months, p = 1.1e-13. Supported but small magnitude (less than 1 day).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_109 is HETEROGENEOUS across racial groups (feature_018) -- e.g., smaller benefit in black or hispanic patients than in white or asian patients.
The PFS benefit of feature_109 is heterogeneous across insurance categories (feature_045) -- e.g., smaller in medicaid/uninsured than in private/medicare.
The PFS benefit of feature_039 is heterogeneous across racial groups.

analyses.code: F-test comparing model with feature_109*C(feature_018) to model with both as main effects only.
analyses.result_summary: F-test for 4 interaction df: F=0.81, p=0.52. Per-stratum coefficients: white +0.157, black +0.136, asian +0.151, hispanic +0.136, other +0.172. Range only 0.04 months across groups; no clinically meaningful heterogeneity. Hypothesis NOT supported.

F-test for 3 interaction df: F=2.51, p=0.057. Per-stratum coefficients: medicare +0.153, private +0.138, medicaid +0.183, uninsured +0.146. Borderline by F-test, but the directional finding goes against the hypothesis: medicaid patients (typically higher disparity risk) have the LARGEST observed benefit. No clinically meaningful disadvantage in any insurance group.
F-test for 4 interaction df: F=1.07, p=0.37. No evidence of racial heterogeneity in the feature_039 effect. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Prevalence of feature_109 (the strongest binary predictor) differs across racial groups in feature_018 (an access-disparity hypothesis: e.g., lower in black or hispanic patients).
Prevalence of feature_109 differs across insurance categories of feature_045 (lower in medicaid/uninsured than in private).
Prevalence of feature_039 differs across racial groups.

analyses.result_summary: Per-race prevalence: asian 10.7%, black 9.4%, hispanic 10.0%, other 9.7%, white 10.0%. Range 1.3 percentage points; chi-square test p~0.2 (not significant). Hypothesis NOT supported: feature_109 is distributed essentially uniformly across racial groups.

Per-insurance prevalence: medicaid 10.3%, medicare 9.9%, private 9.9%, uninsured 10.9%. Chi-square p~0.4. Hypothesis NOT supported. Notably, uninsured patients have the HIGHEST prevalence of feature_109, opposite to typical access-bias expectations.
Per-race prevalence of feature_039: asian 10.6%, black 9.9%, hispanic 9.9%, other 8.6%, white 10.3%. Range ~2 percentage points; chi-square p~0.3. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_057 (PS-like) on pfs_months is amplified at older feature_078 (age x PS interaction is negative).
The negative effect of log(feature_013) (PSA-like) on pfs_months is amplified at older feature_078.
The protective effect of feature_009 (albumin-like) on pfs_months is greater at older feature_078.

analyses.result_summary: feature_078 x feature_057 interaction coefficient = -0.00964 per (year x level), p < 1e-300. Strongly negative. Per-stratum stratified slope of f057: age <55 = -0.92, 55-65 = -1.19, 65-75 = -1.20, >75 = -1.19. Effect grows with age (most pronounced shift between <55 and 55+). Supported.
feature_078 x log_f013 interaction coefficient = -0.00341 per (year x log-unit), p ~ 4e-147. Strongly negative: PSA-like burden hurts older patients more. Supported.
feature_078 x feature_009 interaction coefficient = +0.00439 per (year x unit), p ~ 1e-41. Positive: each unit of feature_009 buys more PFS in older patients. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 was identified as a strong univariate negative predictor in iteration 1 (h2). REFINED hypothesis: feature_051 marks patients with substantially higher feature_013 levels, and once log(feature_013) is in the model, feature_051 has no independent effect on pfs_months.

There is an interaction between feature_051 and log(feature_013) such that the slope of log(feature_013) on pfs_months is less negative when feature_051=1 (i.e., feature_051 partially attenuates the PSA-burden penalty).
The original feature_051 association is therefore confounding rather than a direct treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Among feature_051=0: median feature_013 = 7.4, mean log_f013 = 2.17. Among feature_051=1: median feature_013 = 32.9, mean log_f013 = 3.55. Welch t = 155, p ~ 0. After adding log_f013 to the multivariable model, the feature_051 coefficient drops from -0.376 (p<1e-180) to -0.0005 (p=0.90). Supported: feature_051 was a confounded marker.

log_f013 x feature_051 interaction coefficient = +0.0102 per log-unit, p = 0.003. Direction matches hypothesis (less-negative slope of log_f013 when feature_051=1). Magnitude is small but statistically supported.

Based on h2 (univariate -0.52 mo) collapsing to ~0 after log_f013 adjustment, feature_051's apparent main effect is essentially fully explained by its association with elevated feature_013. The residual signal is a small interaction (h28). Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS containing feature_078, feature_057, feature_109, feature_039, feature_027, log(feature_013), log(feature_006), feature_009 and feature_092 explains > 95% of the variance in pfs_months.
Adding race (feature_018) and insurance (feature_045) to that model adds < 0.1% incremental R^2 (refines h13 and h11/h12).
Across all interactions tested, no race-by-treatment or insurance-by-treatment heterogeneity reaches a clinically meaningful magnitude (effect-size summary).

analyses.code: m = smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_027 + log_f013 + log_f006 + feature_009 + feature_092', data=df).fit(); m.rsquared
analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.96792 in the 9-predictor model; F overall ~ astronomical, all individual coefficient p-values <<0.001. Supported.

analyses.code: m_no.rsquared - m_demo.rsquared; F-test on 7 demographic df
analyses.result_summary: R^2 with demographics = 0.96792, R^2 without = 0.96792. Incremental R^2 = 0.000000 (5 decimal places). Joint F-test F=0.043, p=0.9999. Supported.

analyses.code: Summary of interaction p-values from iter 6: f109*race p=0.52; f109*insurance p=0.057; f039*race p=0.37.
analyses.result_summary: All race-by-treatment interactions p>0.3; the only borderline insurance-by-treatment interaction (p=0.057) had its largest stratum effect (medicaid +0.183) actually FAVORING the typically-disadvantaged group. No interaction effect exceeds 0.05 months across strata. Supported: dataset shows no detectable demographic disparity in treatment effectiveness.
